label: Model Training Plan
description: Plan a rigorous model training run with data, metrics and compute

fields.label: Project name
fields.placeholder: churn-predictor-v2

fields.label: Problem & success criteria
fields.placeholder: Predict 30-day churn; success = recall > 0.7 at precision > 0.4 on the holdout set.

fields.label: Data available
fields.placeholder: ~2M labeled events in BigQuery, class imbalance ~5%, some leakage risk on billing fields.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Inference must be < 50ms; model must be explainable; no PII in features.

template:
You are a senior ML engineer. Write a training plan for "{{projectName}}".

## Objective
{{objective}}
- Task type: {{taskType}}
- Candidate model family: {{modelFamily}}
- Compute budget: {{compute}}

## Data
{{data}}

## Plan to produce
- Data pipeline: splits (train/val/test) with leakage and distribution-shift checks, plus a baseline.
- Feature/representation strategy and preprocessing.
- Training setup: loss, optimizer, schedule, regularization, and a hyperparameter search plan scoped to the compute budget.
- Evaluation: primary and guardrail metrics tied to the success criteria, plus slice/fairness analysis.
{{#if tracking}}- Reproducibility: seeded runs, versioned data/code, and experiment tracking for every run.
{{/if}}- Stopping criteria and a clear go/no-go bar for promotion.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The end-to-end plan with a baseline-first sequence of experiments.
2. The metric definitions and how each maps to the objective.
3. Risks (leakage, imbalance, drift) and concrete mitigations.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: Evaluation Harness
description: Design an automated eval harness for an AI system with scored metrics

fields.label: System under test
fields.placeholder: support-assistant

fields.label: What does the system do?
fields.placeholder: An LLM assistant that answers billing questions using retrieved docs and tools.

fields.label: Run as a CI regression gate
Constraints
fields.placeholder: Eval set ~300 cases; judge cost capped; must catch faithfulness regressions.

template:
You are an AI evaluation specialist. Design an evaluation harness for "{{systemName}}".

## System
{{summary}}
- Type: {{systemType}}
- Quality dimensions: {{dimensions}}
- Scoring method: {{scoring}}

## Harness design
- Dataset: how to build a representative eval set (including hard/edge/adversarial cases) and how to version it.
- Metrics: a precise, reproducible definition and scoring rubric for each dimension.
{{#if scoring == "LLM-as-judge"}}- Judge design: a strict rubric prompt, calibration against human labels, and bias controls.
{{/if}}{{#if scoring == "Hybrid"}}- Combine deterministic checks with an LLM/human judge; calibrate the judge against human labels.
{{/if}}- Aggregation: per-slice scores, confidence intervals, and pass/fail thresholds.
{{#if regression}}- CI integration: a regression gate that blocks merges on score drops, with a stable seed and a flake budget.
{{/if}}
{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The harness architecture and the runnable scoring code/config.
2. The dataset schema and a starter set of example cases.
3. A report format showing scores, regressions and failure exemplars.

Use rigorous, reproducible defaults and explain trade-offs; ask only if blocked.

label: RAG Pipeline
description: Architect a retrieval-augmented generation pipeline end to end

fields.label: Project name
fields.placeholder: docs-copilot

fields.label: Use case & corpus
fields.placeholder: Answer engineering questions over ~50k internal Markdown/PDF docs that change weekly.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Answers must cite sources; p95 < 3s; corpus is confidential, stays in our VPC.

label: AI Agent
description: Specify a tool-using AI agent with safe autonomy and clear stop rules

fields.label: Agent name
fields.placeholder: triage-agent

fields.label: Goal & success criteria
fields.placeholder: Triage inbound bug reports: label, deduplicate, request missing info, and assign.

fields.label: Tools / capabilities
fields.placeholder: GitHub API (read/write issues), search over docs, internal user-lookup endpoint.

options.label: Act with confirmation on risky steps

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Max 15 tool calls per task; never close an issue without confirmation; budget $0.10/task.

template:
You are an AI agent architect. Specify and build the agent "{{agentName}}".

## Goal
{{goal}}
- Autonomy: {{autonomy}}
- Reasoning pattern: {{pattern}}

## Tools
{{tools}}

## Agent design
- A precise system prompt defining role, objective, the tool contract, and exactly when the task is done.
- The control loop using the {{pattern}} pattern, with explicit termination and failure handling (no infinite loops).
- Tool schemas with strict input validation and safe, idempotent side effects.
{{#if memory}}- Memory: what state persists across steps/sessions and how it is read/written.
{{/if}}{{#if guardrails}}- Guardrails: action allowlists, confirmation on irreversible/risky actions, step/cost budgets, and graceful give-up behavior.
{{/if}}- Observability: structured traces of every decision and tool call for debugging.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The system prompt and tool/schema definitions.
2. The runnable agent loop code with guardrails enforced.
3. Test scenarios (happy path, tool failure, adversarial) and how to evaluate them.

Make confident, well-documented decisions; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: Prompt System
description: Engineer a robust, structured prompt for a production LLM task

fields.label: Task name
fields.placeholder: support-reply-drafter

fields.label: What should the LLM do?
fields.placeholder: Draft an empathetic support reply from a ticket plus relevant KB snippets.

fields.label: Inputs available
fields.placeholder: Ticket text, customer tier, KB snippets, prior conversation.

fields.label: Constraints & failure modes
fields.placeholder: Never invent policy; must stay under 150 words; handle non-English input.

label: Fine-Tuning Plan
description: Plan a fine-tuning effort with data curation and clear go/no-go gates

fields.label: Project name
fields.placeholder: tone-adapter

fields.label: Why fine-tune (vs prompting/RAG)?
fields.placeholder: Need consistent brand voice and a fixed JSON schema that prompting can't reliably hit.

fields.label: Training data
fields.placeholder: ~5k production transcripts; need cleaning, dedup, PII scrubbing and a held-out set.

fields.label: Require A/B vs prompted baseline before adoption
Constraints
fields.placeholder: Single A100; must not regress safety; deployable as a LoRA adapter.

template:
You are an ML engineer specializing in model adaptation. Write a fine-tuning plan for "{{projectName}}".

## Objective & justification
{{objective}}
- Base model class: {{baseModel}}
- Method: {{method}}

## Training data
{{dataSource}}

## Plan to produce
- A justification that fine-tuning beats prompting/RAG for this objective, or a recommendation to stop.
- Data curation: cleaning, dedup, PII scrubbing, formatting to the training schema, and a frozen held-out set.
- Training config for {{method}}: hyperparameters, run scope, and overfitting safeguards.
- Evaluation: task metrics plus regression checks on general capability and safety.
{{#if evalAgainstBaseline}}- A blind A/B vs the best prompted baseline as the explicit adoption gate.
{{/if}}- Deployment & rollback: how the tuned model/adapter ships and how to revert.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The end-to-end plan with a clear go/no-go decision gate.
2. The data spec and example formatted training records.
3. Risks (overfitting, capability/safety regression, data leakage) with mitigations.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: Data Labeling Guide
description: Write precise labeling guidelines and a QA process for annotators

fields.label: Dataset / task name
fields.placeholder: intent-classification-v3

fields.label: What is the labeled data for?
fields.placeholder: Training an intent classifier for support chat; 12 intent classes plus 'other'.

fields.label: Label schema / categories
fields.placeholder: billing, cancel, refund, technical, account, ... ; one primary label per item.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Annotators non-expert; PII must be flagged not transcribed; target IAA kappa > 0.7.

template:
You are a data annotation lead. Write a labeling guide for "{{datasetName}}".

## Purpose
{{purpose}}
- Annotation type: {{labelType}}

## Label schema
{{schema}}

## Guide to produce
- A crisp definition of each label/category with inclusion and exclusion criteria.
- Decision rules for ambiguous cases and a deterministic tie-breaking order.
{{#if edgeCases}}- Worked examples: clear positives, clear negatives, and tricky edge cases with the correct label and reasoning.
{{/if}}- Annotator instructions: workflow, what to skip/flag, and how to mark uncertainty.
- QA process: {{qa}}, including how disagreements are adjudicated and inter-annotator agreement is measured and acted on.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The complete guidelines document, written for the stated annotators.
2. A labeled gold/example set illustrating each rule.
3. The QA plan with agreement metrics and a path for guideline updates.

Be unambiguous and operational; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: Model Card
description: Write an honest model card covering use, limits, evaluation and ethics

fields.label: Model name & version
fields.placeholder: churn-predictor v2.1

fields.label: Intended use & users
fields.placeholder: Scores account churn risk for the CS team to prioritize outreach; not for automated cancellation.

fields.label: Training data & evaluation
fields.placeholder: ~2M events 2023-2024; eval on a time-based holdout; metrics: AUC 0.86, recall 0.71.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Must follow the Model Cards format; flag any fairness gaps honestly; no PII in features.

template:
You are an ML governance specialist. Write a model card for "{{modelName}}" for an audience of {{audience}}.

## Intended use
{{purpose}}
- Model type: {{modelType}}

## Training data & evaluation
{{trainingData}}

## Card to produce
- Cover these sections explicitly: {{sections}}.
- State performance with the metric definitions and report it broken down by meaningful slices, not just an aggregate.
- Be candid about limitations, known failure modes, and out-of-scope / prohibited uses.
{{#if regulated}}- Treat this as high-stakes: document fairness analysis, human-oversight requirements, and recourse for affected users.
{{/if}}- Note data provenance, consent/licensing, and maintenance ownership.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The complete model card in Markdown with the chosen sections.
2. A slice-performance table and a plain-language summary of limitations.
3. Honest open risks and what would need re-evaluation before broader use.

Be truthful over flattering; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: ML Monitoring Plan
description: Design drift, quality and performance monitoring for a deployed model

fields.label: Deployed model
fields.placeholder: fraud-scorer v4

fields.label: How it serves & what it predicts
fields.placeholder: Real-time fraud score on each transaction; ~800 rps; label arrives 1-14 days later via chargebacks.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: Reuse Prometheus+Grafana; alert fatigue is a concern; PII must not leave the VPC.

template:
You are an MLOps engineer. Design a monitoring plan for the deployed model "{{modelName}}".

## Serving context
{{summary}}
- Serving mode: {{serving}}
- Signals to monitor: {{signals}}

## Monitoring design
- For each signal: the exact metric, how it is computed, the baseline/reference window, and a defensible alert threshold.
- Distinguish input drift from genuine quality degradation; don't alert on noise.
{{#if labelDelay}}- Handle delayed ground truth: proxy/leading metrics now plus a backfilled quality metric once labels land.
{{/if}}- Per-slice monitoring so a regression on a small but important segment is not masked by the aggregate.
{{#if retrain}}- Concrete retraining and rollback triggers tied to thresholds, with a guarded promotion path.
{{/if}}- Dashboards and on-call alerts that point to a likely cause and a runbook.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The monitoring spec (metrics, windows, thresholds) as config where possible.
2. Dashboard and alert definitions plus a triage runbook.
3. The retrain/rollback decision logic and known blind spots.

Use rigorous, low-noise defaults; ask only if genuinely blocked.

label: Feature Engineering Plan
description: Plan features, transformations and a leakage-safe pipeline for a model

fields.label: Project / model
fields.placeholder: ltv-prediction

fields.label: Prediction target & grain
fields.placeholder: Predict 12-month customer LTV at the account level, scored monthly.

fields.label: Raw data sources
fields.placeholder: Events table, billing, CRM, support tickets; mix of categorical, numeric, text and timestamps.

fields.label: Constraints
fields.placeholder: No future data leakage; features must be computable at serve time within 50ms; explainable.

template:
You are a senior ML engineer specializing in feature engineering. Plan the features for "{{projectName}}".

## Objective
{{objective}}
- Downstream model family: {{modelFamily}}
- Techniques to consider: {{techniques}}

## Raw data
{{rawData}}

## Plan to produce
- A candidate feature list grouped by source, each with the transformation, the rationale, and the expected signal.
- Point-in-time correctness: how every feature is computed using only data available at prediction time (no target/temporal leakage).
- Encoding, scaling, and missing-value handling chosen appropriately for the {{modelFamily}} model.
{{#if featureStore}}- Train/serve parity: a single feature definition reused offline and online via a feature store, with backfill.
{{/if}}- Feature selection/validation: how to test importance, redundancy, and stability before adoption.

{{#if constraints}}## Constraints
{{constraints}}

{{/if}}## Deliverables
1. The feature catalog with transformations and leakage notes.
2. The pipeline design (offline + serving) and the parity strategy.
3. A validation plan and the riskiest features to scrutinize first.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if genuinely blocked.
